title: quercus hintonii
intro: Quercus hintonii, also known as Hinton's oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae, native to Mexico. The bark of Quercus hintonii contains tannins, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and astringent effects. The health benefits of Quercus hintonii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus hintonii can be found in tea, tincture, or capsule form, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
